context.meal: breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, tartar sauce

episode_text:
day=29; meal=breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, tartar sauce; setting=Personal; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=1 min; occlusion_relevance=do not consider occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is fruit, dessert, or another cold-served item AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused THEN no microwave BECAUSE observed cold items were explicitly preferred without reheating across contexts, including repeated laptop-right fruit meals. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a hot savory meal such as buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, chicken breast strips, hash browns, or fish bites with potatoes THEN 1 min, 2 min, or 3 min BECAUSE personal hot-meal evidence consistently supports reheating but now shows that the preferred amount can range from light to strong warming in quiet personal settings. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a hot savory meal with a sauce or dip component such as fish bites with tartar sauce THEN 1 min or 2 min BECAUSE the newest personal morning meal suggests a milder reheating preference for this kind of meal than the stronger 3 min pattern seen elsewhere. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop, including with the laptop on the left or right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a hot savory meal such as chicken, broccoli, potatoes, hash browns, chicken nuggets, or General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN 3 min BECAUSE laptop hot-meal evidence repeatedly favored substantial reheating. IF dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV, including when the TV is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a hot savory meal such as chicken, broccoli, hash browns, bite-sized sandwiches, pizza, brownies with sauce, or fish bites with potatoes THEN 3 min BECAUSE TV meals consistently show a strong preference for thoroughly reheated food. IF dining context is social with a person in front, on the right, or on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a hot savory meal such as chicken nuggets, chicken breast strips, hash browns, broccoli, ketchup, or General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated social-meal evidence shows a moderated reheating preference relative to the stronger 3 min pattern in solitary screen-focused contexts.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a front-facing focal activity such as watching TV, working on a laptop, or interacting with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred preserving the front view in front-focused contexts, and front-social evidence matches the same pattern. IF dining context includes a focal activity to the right such as watching TV on the right, working on a laptop on the right, or eating socially with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN minimize right occlusion or do not consider occlusion BECAUSE right-focused contexts usually favored protecting the right-side view, and repeated laptop-right fruit meals remain a simpler-food exception where occlusion concern was relaxed. IF dining context includes a focal activity to the left such as watching TV on the left, working on a laptop on the left, or eating socially with a person on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE left-focused evidence across entertainment, work, and social meals supports protecting the left-side view when attention or companionship is there. IF dining context is personal with no salient external focal target AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE repeated personal meals, including the newest personal fish-bites meal, support the pattern that occlusion concerns are mainly tied to protecting a specific line of sight. IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a simple cold meal such as fruit THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE one observed social-front fruit meal relaxed the usual front-protection pattern. Across observed evidence, the tie-break is to protect the most behaviorally important line of sight, but simple cold fruit meals can weaken that preference.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal, social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN slow BECAUSE the user has usually selected the slower movement option, fitting a comfort-first interaction style, and the newest personal meal is consistent with that pattern. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a simple cold fruit meal THEN medium has been observed once BECAUSE one earlier laptop-fruit episode showed a modest increase in speed, but the broader pattern still favors slow. Across observed evidence, the tie-break remains comfort-first, with medium appearing as a narrow exception rather than a stable shift.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition such as fruit pieces, strawberries, cantaloupe, banana, watermelon, broccoli, brownies, fish bites, or other compact hot solid foods AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social, task-focused work contexts, or TV contexts THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the higher-acquisition-success option across several feeding meals, including repeated laptop-right fruit meals. IF food is a long, narrow, elongated, nugget-like, strip-like, wedge-like, piecewise hand-food item, or a mixed hot entrée where the user has repeatedly accepted elongated-bite style acquisition, such as chicken strips, chicken breast strips, bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets, buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, General Tso's chicken pieces, bite-sized pizza, or fish bites in a personal meal AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, entertainment-focused, social, or task-focused THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE repeated selections with elongated or discrete hand-food-style items, now including the newest personal fish-bites meal, indicate that food geometry can override the general preference for acquisition success. Across observed evidence, food geometry is the main driver of this preference.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food, including simple fruit meals and substantial hot meals, THEN yes or no BECAUSE social evidence shows both reliability-seeking confirmation and skipping confirmation to reduce interaction burden, so social context alone does not force confirmation. IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a substantial or potentially less straightforward hot meal THEN yes has been common BECAUSE several social meals favored explicit confirmation for reliability. IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a straightforward hot finger-food-style meal such as chicken nuggets with broccoli THEN no has also been observed BECAUSE the user sometimes accepts lower interaction burden even around other people when bite acquisition is likely to be straightforward. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a straightforward hot finger-food-style or bite-sized meal such as buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, chicken breast strips, hash browns, or fish bites with potatoes THEN yes or no BECAUSE personal evidence shows both skipping confirmation to reduce interaction burden and requesting confirmation to ensure reliable bite acquisition for similar hot meals, with the newest personal fish-bites meal favoring confirmation. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop, including with the laptop on the left or right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is straightforward fork-fed food, including strawberries, mixed fruit, cantaloupe, bananas, watermelon, brownies, bite-sized pizza, chicken nuggets, or other simple hot solids, THEN no BECAUSE the user often skipped confirmation in divided-attention contexts to reduce interruption for simpler foods, and repeated laptop-right fruit meals reinforce that pattern. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a more substantial hot savory meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN yes BECAUSE laptop-work hot-meal evidence shows that reliability can override interruption reduction for heavier meals. IF dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a more substantial or messier hot fork-fed meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli or fish bites with potatoes and tartar sauce THEN yes BECAUSE observed TV evidence indicates that for heavier hot meals the user favors reliability over reduced interaction. IF dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simpler fork-fed item such as fruit or bite-sized pizza THEN no BECAUSE prior TV evidence showed reduced-interruption preferences for simpler foods. Across observed contexts, the tie-break is reliability for heavier or less certain meals, but lower interaction burden for straightforward foods and divided-attention settings; social meals are mixed rather than uniformly confirmation-seeking.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is any AND dining context is any context THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot reliably lean forward due to limited trunk control, and they consistently selected inside-mouth transfer across observed meals.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer AND affective_state is any AND dining context is any THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user consistently selected inside-mouth transfer and their physical profile supports that mode.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a substantial hot meal such as buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, chicken breast strips, hash browns, or fish bites with potatoes THEN speech or speech + LED BECAUSE personal hot-meal evidence favors salient cues, but the newest personal meal shows the user may simplify to speech-only rather than requiring multimodal signaling. IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED or speech + LED BECAUSE social evidence includes both quiet visual-only signaling and multimodal signaling, with some meals favoring extra salience and others minimizing conversational disruption. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop, including with the laptop on the left or right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED or speech + LED BECAUSE work-context evidence is mixed: many laptop meals favored quiet visual signaling, but repeated laptop-right fruit meals show that the user can also choose multimodal salience while working. IF dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV, including with the TV in front, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED or speech + LED BECAUSE TV evidence includes both multimodal and visual-only signaling. Across observed evidence, the stable tie-break is that personal meals favor salient auditory involvement, while social, work, and TV contexts permit either quieter visual-only or more noticeable multimodal cues depending on whether the user prioritizes quietness or noticeability.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND dining context is personal or social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN open mouth or autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons, and non-screen settings show both explicit low-effort readiness signaling and automatic progression, with the newest personal meal again favoring automation. IF feeding task AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN open mouth has been common BECAUSE several social meals used explicit user-driven readiness despite the effort being low. IF feeding task AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN autocontinue has also been observed BECAUSE some social meals show the user may prefer less active signaling even in social settings. IF feeding task AND dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused such as working on a laptop or watching TV, including with the laptop on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred more automation when attention was occupied, and repeated laptop-right fruit meals reinforce that pattern. Across observed contexts, the tie-break is toward automation under divided attention and sometimes even in personal or social settings, while open-mouth remains the main explicit low-effort option when the user wants direct control without buttons.

IF drinking task AND dining context is personal or social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any drink THEN open mouth or autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons, and non-screen evidence includes both explicit low-effort initiation and automatic initiation, with the newest personal meal favoring automation. IF drinking task AND dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused such as working on a laptop or watching TV, including with the laptop on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred automatic initiation when attention was divided, and repeated laptop-right meals are consistent with that pattern. Across observed contexts, the tie-break is explicit low-effort initiation in some non-screen settings but more automation when the user is distracted or prefers less active signaling.

IF mouth wiping task AND dining context is personal or social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any wiping need THEN open mouth or autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons, and non-screen evidence includes both explicit low-effort initiation and automatic initiation, with the newest personal meal favoring automation. IF mouth wiping task AND dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused such as working on a laptop or watching TV, including with the laptop on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any wiping need THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred automatic initiation when attention was divided, and repeated laptop-right meals are consistent with that pattern. Across observed contexts, the tie-break is explicit low-effort initiation in some non-screen settings but more automation when the user is distracted or prefers less active signaling.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a substantial hot meal such as buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, chicken breast strips, hash browns, or fish bites with potatoes THEN speech or speech + LED BECAUSE personal hot-meal evidence favors salient completion cues, but the newest personal meal shows the user may simplify to speech-only rather than requiring multimodal signaling. IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED or speech + LED BECAUSE social evidence includes both quiet visual-only completion cues and multimodal completion cues, with some meals favoring extra salience and others minimizing conversational disruption. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop, including with the laptop on the left or right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED or speech + LED BECAUSE work-context evidence is mixed: many laptop meals favored quiet visual-only completion signaling, but repeated laptop-right fruit meals show that the user can also choose multimodal completion cues while working. IF dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV, including with the TV in front, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED or speech + LED BECAUSE TV evidence includes both multimodal and visual-only completion signaling. Across observed evidence, personal meals favor auditory salience, while social, work, and TV contexts are mixed between visual-only and multimodal cues depending on whether quietness or noticeability is prioritized.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any context THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable low-effort completion method, which fits their inability to reliably press buttons.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or head gestures and consistently selected automatic progression for drinking.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or head gestures and consistently selected automatic progression for wiping.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a person or focal activity that the user wants to keep visible, including in front, to the right, or to the left, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN yes or no BECAUSE the user often preferred reducing prolonged visual obstruction in social, laptop-working, and TV contexts, but some meals show that they may also skip retracting when efficiency matters. IF dining context is personal with no salient external focal target AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN no BECAUSE repeated personal meals, including the newest personal fish-bites meal, show the user is willing to skip retracting to reduce meal time when view obstruction is less important. IF dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused with a salient screen target AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN yes has been common BECAUSE preserving visibility has repeatedly mattered in those contexts. IF dining context is social with a person in front, on the right, or on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN yes has been common but no has also been observed BECAUSE most social meals favored visibility protection, while some social evidence indicates that efficiency can sometimes override that concern. Across observed evidence, the tie-break is to retract when preserving visibility is important, but this preference is not absolute and can yield to efficiency.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral THEN less or do not dip BECAUSE personal evidence shows both limited dipping and complete avoidance, with the newest personal fish-bites meal with tartar sauce favoring no dipping and reinforcing a generally restrained mess-avoidance style. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral THEN less or do not dip BECAUSE social evidence repeatedly favored limited dipping to reduce messiness, and some social meals chose to avoid dipping entirely. IF food includes a dip, sauce, whipped cream, or other optional topping AND dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop, including with the laptop on the right, AND affective_state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user chose to avoid dipping or extra coating in observed laptop meals, including repeated laptop-right fruit meals, reinforcing a strong mess-avoidance preference when occupied. IF food includes a dip or sauce or topping AND dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV AND affective_state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE observed TV meals were explicitly set to no dipping, extending the pattern that divided-attention contexts favor minimizing mess. IF food is a sauced hot meal where dipping is unnecessary, such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli, OR a hot meal without a separate dip such as chicken breast strips and hash browns, AND dining context is personal or social AND affective_state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no additional dipping when sauce was already part of the meal or when no separate dip was used. IF food has no dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not applicable and observed no-sauce meals also used do-not-dip. Across observed evidence, the tie-break is mess minimization, especially in social and divided-attention contexts.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 10 sec, 100 sec, or 1000 sec BECAUSE personal meals now show a wide range from very short to very long waits, indicating that quiet personal settings can support either rapid progression or extended intervention time depending on the meal. IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a straightforward hot meal in a more efficiency-oriented episode THEN 10 sec has been observed BECAUSE the newest personal fish-bites meal shows the user can prefer very fast automatic continuation even in a quiet setting. IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 100 sec or 1000 sec BECAUSE social meals often used a moderate wait, but some social evidence shows the user may also want a very long intervention window. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop, including with the laptop on the left or right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 1000 sec for strongly attention-capturing work contexts, but 100 sec has also been observed for a simpler cold-fruit laptop meal BECAUSE the user usually prefers a long intervention window while working, though some straightforward meals tolerated a shorter moderate wait. IF dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 100 sec or 1000 sec BECAUSE TV meals show both a moderate and a very long wait preference. Across observed contexts, the tie-break is a longer intervention window when the user may be distracted or slower to respond through assistive interaction, but personal meals can shift toward much shorter waits when the user prioritizes efficiency.
